The system must allow users to register via email and password. The registration form will include full name, email, password, and optional GitHub username. Passwords are hashed using ASP.NET Core Identity and stored securely in SQL Server.
The system must support GitHub OAuth2 login to simplify onboarding and link code submissions automatically. OAuth tokens will be stored securely (encrypted) in the database.
Upon registration, the system must send a verification email using the SendGrid API before account activation.
Users must log in using either email/password or GitHub OAuth. JWT tokens will be issued by the .NET API for authenticated access.
Users can request a password reset link, sent via email. The token is valid for 10 minutes.
Users can update personal information (name, GitHub username, profile picture). Changes are reflected immediately in the database.
The system must distinguish between Admin and Learner roles using claims-based authorization.
Active sessions are cached using Redis, with automatic expiration and rate limiting to prevent abuse.
A learner can begin an adaptive assessment (30 questions). The difficulty adjusts dynamically based on previous answers.
The assessment uses a curated question bank stored in SQL Server. Questions are categorized by difficulty and topic.
The backend (.NET) calculates difficulty progression and selects the next question dynamically based on prior performance.
The assessment is time-bound (e.g., 40 minutes). Unanswered questions are auto-submitted.
After completion, the system computes the learner’s skill level and stores the score, completion time, and performance summary in SQL Server.
The Python FastAPI service may later enhance this module by generating personalized advice (“You’re strong in data structures, weak in security”).
The frontend displays a result dashboard showing percentage scores and categorized skill levels.
Users can retake the assessment after 30 days .
Based on assessment results, the system generates a personalized learning path from predefined templates (Full Stack, Backend, etc.).
Each user’s path is stored in the LearningPath table, linked to their UserId.
Each path includes ordered tasks linked through the PathTasks junction table.
Users can manually add a recommended task from feedback to their learning path.
The backend calculates path progress (e.g., tasks completed / total tasks) and updates in real-time.
Users can re-order or remove tasks within their path; updates are persisted.
Accept code submissions by linking GitHub repositories and automatically fetching contents via GitHub API.
GitHub REST API, OAuth token management
Accept direct file uploads (ZIP) for users without GitHub or preferring local development.
Azure Blob Storage, file validation service
Capture and store metadata for each submission including status, timestamps, and task linkage.
SQL Server Submissions table, audit logging
Background Worker fetches GitHub repos or uploaded files, extracts them, and handles cleanup.
.NET Background Worker, GitHub API, temp file isolation
Execute ESLint, SonarQube, Bandit, etc. inside Docker containers with timeouts and resource limits.

SQL Server StaticAnalysis table, JSON parsing
Worker sends code + static analysis results + task context to Python FastAPI microservice.
FastAPI AI microservice, HTTP schema validation
Store AI-generated scores, raw JSON feedback, timestamps, and model metadata.
Combine static + AI output into a unified report with insights and recommendations.
Manage queued submission processing with durable, reliable job handling.
Support multiple attempts per task and track version history and improvement.
Provide real-time updates, notifications, and fail/retry options.
Email service, notification system, status polling API

Frontend renders feedback in a clear, organized, interactive interface with actionable insights.
System generates 3–5 task recommendations based on skill gaps and stores them per submission.
Task database, AI recommendation logic, Recommendations table

AI resource recommendation logic, Resources table, link validation service

Charting library, analytics aggregation service, PDF export tools

Core API endpoints must remain responsive under normal and peak load conditions.
Full code analysis pipeline (static + AI) must complete within acceptable time limits.
Static ≤3 min, AI ≤2 min, Total ≤5 min typical / ≤10 min max
SQL queries must be optimized through indexing and proper querying strategies.
Frontend must load quickly and remain visually stable for a pleasant UX.
System must handle increasing numbers of simultaneous active users smoothly.
Phase 1: 100 users, Phase 2: 500 users, Phase 3: 2000+ users
Frequently accessed data must be served from cache to reduce DB load.
Backend API must support horizontal scaling via stateless design and load balancing.
Auto-scale when CPU ≥75% or queue depth ≥50; 2–10 instances (Phase 1–2), unlimited Phase 3
Worker service must scale to handle increased submission volume.
Scale-out when queue depth ≥20; ≥5 parallel jobs per instance; up to 20 workers
SQL Server must scale using read replicas, connection pooling, and table partitioning.
Read replicas for analytics; monthly partitioning when >1M records; pool size 20–200
Blob storage must scale elastically with no capacity limits.
Unlimited Azure Blob capacity; file limit 50MB; auto-tiering based on usage
System must support large increases in concurrent learners.
Phase 1: 100 users, Phase 2: 500 users, Phase 3: 2000+ users
Scaling events should occur quickly to avoid backlog buildup.
Queue processing must remain stable during peak hours.
Maintain queue at <100 pending jobs during load spikes
Platform must maintain high availability for global learners.
System continues core functionality even when dependent services fail.
AI failure → show static analysis; Email failure → queue; Cache failure → fallback to DB
Critical data must be backed up and recoverable within strict time objectives.
RPO ≤ 1 hour; RTO ≤ 4 hours; Daily full + 6-hour incremental backups
Background jobs must retry automatically upon transient failures.
Platform must recover from major cloud outages with minimal downtime.
No job should be lost during worker restarts or failures.
Durable queues (Azure Service Bus Dead-letter support)
Maintenance must not disrupt global users and should be communicated early.
Scheduled 7 days ahead, 2–4 AM UTC window
Authentication must follow industry-standard security practices.
PBKDF2 (100k iterations), JWT RS256, MFA for admins
Role-based access must restrict all sensitive operations.
All sensitive data encrypted at rest and in transit.
TLS 1.3, SQL TDE, AES-256, Azure Key Vault
Prevent injection attacks via strict validation.
Uploaded files must be scanned and isolated securely.
MIME validation, ClamAV scan, sandbox execution
Redis sliding-window limiter (100 req/min), 5 auth attempts/15m
Protect GitHub OAuth tokens from exposure.
AES-256 encrypted storage via Key Vault
Log all high-risk actions for audit & detection.
APIs must follow secure communication and error-handling guidelines.
HTTPS-only, no sensitive data in error responses
AI responses must be sanitized for safety and compliance.
Platform must be fully responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop.
Interface must support accessible use for users with disabilities.
Screen readers, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation
Clear layouts, breadcrumbs, and menus must simplify task flows.
New users must be guided smoothly through platform features.
Users must receive clear, actionable error and success messages.
System must be prepared for multilingual UI support.
Mobile UI must be optimized for touch interactions and small screens.
Provide consistent loading indicators for slow operations.
System must follow clean, modular architecture with clear separation of concerns.
Code must follow strict quality standards and best practices.
Maintain comprehensive automated tests for reliability and refactoring safety.
≥80% unit test coverage, integration + E2E tests
All APIs must be fully documented and explorable interactively.
Follow structured branching and review practices to ensure code quality.
Git Flow + protected branches + PR approvals
Automated pipelines must handle build, test, and deployment workflows.
System must provide deep visibility into logs, performance, and tracing.
Azure App Insights + structured JSON logs
AI integration must support provider swapping with zero refactoring.
Schema changes must be safe, reversible, and version-controlled.
Documentation must remain accurate and updated throughout development.
Living documentation via Notion + quarterly audits
Backend APIs must follow REST principles for cross-platform compatibility.
System must integrate securely with GitHub, SendGrid, Stripe, and others.
System must process external webhook events reliably and securely.
Users/admins must export/import data in standard formats.
.NET backend must interact seamlessly with Python FastAPI AI service.
Platform must use cloud blob storage for large files and reports.
API and Worker communicate asynchronously using message queues.
Centralized logs/metrics collected from all microservices.
Users must be able to access, export, delete, and control their personal data.
User data must be retained and deleted according to legal and platform policies.
User-submitted code must remain private and never used for AI training without consent.
All AI feedback must include clear disclosure and adhere to ethical guidelines.
Platform must follow accessibility standards to support users with disabilities.
Clear privacy and cookie policies must be provided with user consent controls.
All admin changes and sensitive user actions must be logged for accountability.
Cloud infrastructure must be cost-efficient without affecting performance.
Auto-scale down off-peak, reserved instances, right-sizing, storage tiering
AI usage must be optimized to reduce token consumption and API costs.
Prompt shortening, response length limits, caching, batch processing
Database must use optimal tiers and archival strategies to reduce cost.
Scale tiers as needed, archive old data, caching heavy queries
Blob storage costs must be minimized while retaining required data.
Automatic alerts must notify when cloud spending exceeds thresholds.
Azure Cost Alerts + monthly cost reviews
Queue throughput must be optimized to avoid cost spikes due to over-scaling.
Balanced worker scaling + queue depth monitoring
Optimize external API calls (GitHub, SendGrid, Stripe) to control cost.
Retry logic, caching, reduced polling frequency
Relational schema, Entity Framework Core ORM
Break complex operations into understandable functional steps
Highlight where data is stored and how processes use it
Show how user input transforms into system output
Identify touchpoints between internal processes and external services
Ensure that all functional requirements have corresponding processes
3rd Normal Form (3NF) with selective denormalization for performance
Immutable audit log table for compliance
IsActive / IsDeleted flags instead of hard deletes
CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, timestamps for all entities
